title: Jakarta EE (JEE) – Keywords & Terms to Remember
description: Quick-reference glossary for every important keyword, annotation, interface, concept, and tool in the JEE course. Chapters follow course order; within each chapter terms are sorted by category (specification → concept → component → annotation → api → reference → tool) then alphabetically. Each entry includes a plain-English definition and a memory hook.

terms.definition: The smallest Jakarta EE profile, introduced in Jakarta EE 10. Contains CDI Lite, JAX-RS, JSON-P, JSON-B, Jakarta Annotations, and Interceptors. Designed for microservices and ahead-of-time (native) compilation.
terms.memory_hook: Core Profile = minimal and lean. No Faces, no JPA, no EJB. Perfect for microservices.

terms.definition: The complete Jakarta EE specification set including all APIs: EJB, JMS, Batch, SOAP (JAX-WS), Connector Architecture, and more. Requires a full application server like GlassFish or WildFly.
terms.memory_hook: Full Platform = everything included. If in doubt, this profile has it.

terms.definition: The open-source enterprise Java platform, formerly Java EE, now governed by the Eclipse Foundation. An umbrella of dozens of specifications for building distributed, secure, and scalable enterprise applications.
terms.memory_hook: Jakarta EE = Java's enterprise platform, rebranded and open-sourced in 2019 under Eclipse.

terms.definition: A focused subset of Jakarta EE for web application development. Includes Servlet, JSF, JPA, CDI, Bean Validation, JAX-RS, JSON-P, and JSON-B. Excludes EJB remoting, JMS, and Batch.
terms.memory_hook: Web Profile = the common web stack. No messaging or remoting.

terms.definition: Metadata attached to Java code elements (class, method, field, parameter). In Jakarta EE, annotations replace most XML configuration and are processed at compile time, deployment time, or at runtime via reflection.
terms.memory_hook: @ = the primary way to configure Jakarta EE behaviour. No XML needed in most cases.

terms.definition: The server layer containing business rules: CDI Beans, Session Beans (EJB), Message-Driven Beans, JTA-managed transactions, and Batch jobs.
terms.memory_hook: Middle Tier = where the actual work happens. Business rules, transactions, messaging.

terms.definition: The outermost application layer, running on the user's machine: Web Browsers (HTML/JS) and App Clients (Swing, CLI). Communicates via HTTP/HTTPS or RMI/IIOP.
terms.memory_hook: Client Tier = what runs on the user's device. Browser or desktop app.

terms.definition: A managed runtime environment, provided by the application server, that hosts application components. It supplies cross-cutting services automatically — dependency injection, transaction management, security, lifecycle callbacks — so developers focus only on business logic.
terms.memory_hook: Container = the server-side box your code runs in. It handles all the plumbing.

terms.definition: Also called 'Programming by Exception'. The platform provides sensible defaults for all common scenarios; developers only write configuration for the exceptional cases that deviate from those defaults.
terms.memory_hook: Convention over Config = defaults handle most cases. You only configure the exceptions.

terms.definition: The bottom layer consisting of external systems: relational and NoSQL databases (via JPA/JDBC), legacy EIS systems (via JCA), and JMS message queues and topics.
terms.memory_hook: Data Tier = databases, queues, and external enterprise systems.

terms.definition: An XML configuration file deployed alongside the module (e.g. web.xml, ejb-jar.xml, persistence.xml). Can override annotations and is required for some advanced configurations.
terms.memory_hook: Deployment Descriptor = the XML alternative/override for annotations. web.xml is the most common.

terms.definition: The neutral open-source foundation that governs both Jakarta EE and Eclipse MicroProfile. Runs the Jakarta EE Specification Process through the Jakarta EE Working Group.
terms.memory_hook: Eclipse Foundation = the neutral home for Jakarta EE and MicroProfile standards.

terms.definition: Eclipse Enterprise for Java — the Eclipse Foundation top-level project that governs and stewards the Jakarta EE specification process and its associated repositories.
terms.memory_hook: EE4J is the Eclipse project umbrella. Jakarta EE lives inside it.

terms.definition: In September 2019, Oracle transferred Java EE to the Eclipse Foundation under the EE4J project. All package namespaces changed from javax.* to jakarta.*. Jakarta EE 8 was the first release under Eclipse.
terms.memory_hook: 2019: Java EE becomes Jakarta EE. javax.* → jakarta.*. The most visible breaking change.

terms.definition: The server layer inside the Web Container. Handles HTTP concerns: Servlets, JSF pages, JAX-RS REST resources, WebSocket endpoints, JSON-P/B processing.
terms.memory_hook: Web Tier = everything HTTP-facing inside the server.

terms.definition: Manages CDI beans across the whole application, spanning both the Web and EJB tiers. Provides dependency injection, scopes, interceptors, decorators, and events.
terms.memory_hook: CDI Container = the glue. Crosses all tiers and wires everything together.

terms.definition: Hosts Enterprise JavaBeans: Stateless, Stateful, and Singleton Session Beans, and Message-Driven Beans. Provides pooling, transaction management, security, and optional remoting.
terms.memory_hook: EJB Container = business logic container. Pooling and transactions come for free.

terms.definition: Hosts web components: Servlets, JSF pages, JAX-RS resources, and WebSocket endpoints. Manages the HTTP request/response lifecycle. Apache Tomcat is a well-known standalone web container.
terms.memory_hook: Web Container = the HTTP-facing container. Tomcat is a standalone version.

terms.definition: Enterprise Archive. Bundles multiple Jakarta EE modules (.war web modules and .jar EJB modules) into one deployable unit for a full-platform application server.
terms.memory_hook: .ear = the big envelope. Use when you need .war and EJB .jars together.

terms.definition: Java Archive. Used for EJB modules, shared libraries, Jakarta Batch jobs, and standalone Java SE applications.
terms.memory_hook: .jar = general-purpose Java bundle. EJB module or shared library.

terms.definition: Web Application Archive. Packages a web module containing Servlets, JSF pages, JAX-RS resources, and supporting classes. Deployed to the Web Container.
terms.memory_hook: .war = the web app bundle. What you deploy for a Servlet/JSF/REST app.

terms.definition: The official open-source reference implementation of Jakarta EE, maintained by the Eclipse Foundation. Used by specification authors to validate compliance.
terms.memory_hook: GlassFish = the official Jakarta EE reference server.

terms.definition: A production-ready Jakarta EE application server derived from GlassFish, with extra features and support. Payara Micro is the stripped-down edition for microservices and embedded deployments.
terms.memory_hook: Payara = GlassFish made production-ready. Payara Micro = microservices edition.

terms.definition: Red Hat's open-source Jakarta EE application server (formerly JBoss AS). The community upstream of the commercial JBoss EAP product.
terms.memory_hook: WildFly = Red Hat's Jakarta EE server. Commercial version = JBoss EAP.

terms.definition: The default public remote Maven repository. Maven downloads any artifact not found in the local repository from here automatically.
terms.memory_hook: Central Repository = the internet's shared Maven artifact store.

terms.definition: Controls when a dependency appears on the classpath and whether it is included in the packaged artifact. Key scopes: compile (default), test, provided (server supplies at runtime), runtime.
terms.memory_hook: Scope = 'when is this jar needed?' Most important for Jakarta EE: 'provided' for server APIs.

terms.definition: The 'GAV' coordinates that uniquely identify every Maven artifact. groupId = organisation/namespace, artifactId = project/module name, version = release identifier.
terms.memory_hook: GAV = the unique address of a dependency. Organisation + name + version.

terms.definition: A directory on the developer's machine (default: ~/.m2/repository) where Maven caches every downloaded artifact and every locally-installed project artifact.
terms.memory_hook: Local repo = your machine's artifact cache. Avoids re-downloading the same jar.

terms.definition: An ordered sequence of build phases: validate → compile → test → package → verify → install → deploy. Running any phase automatically runs all earlier phases.
terms.memory_hook: Lifecycle: Validate, Compile, Test, Package, Verify, Install, Deploy. Earlier phases always run first.

terms.definition: The dependency is on the compile-time classpath but NOT packaged in the final .war/.ear. The application server supplies it at runtime.
terms.memory_hook: 'provided' = compile but don't bundle. Use for all jakarta.* APIs.

terms.definition: Maven artifact for the full Jakarta EE platform API (all specs). groupId=jakarta.platform. Always use scope=provided.
terms.memory_hook: Full platform: artifactId=jakarta.jakartaee-api, scope=provided.

terms.definition: Maven artifact for the Jakarta EE Core Profile API. Use for microservices targeting the Core Profile.
terms.memory_hook: Core Profile: swap to 'jakartaee-core-api'.

terms.definition: Maven artifact for the Jakarta EE Web Profile API only. Use when building web-only applications.
terms.memory_hook: Web Profile: swap 'jakartaee-api' → 'jakartaee-web-api'.

terms.definition: The most common Maven command. Runs validate → compile → test → package, producing a .war or .jar in the target/ directory.
terms.memory_hook: 'mvn package' = build your deployable artifact. Output lands in target/.

terms.definition: Project Object Model — the single Maven configuration file at the root of every project. Declares dependencies, plugins, packaging type (jar/war/ear), build profiles, and parent POM inheritance.
terms.memory_hook: pom.xml = Maven's project passport. One per project, everything declared here.

terms.definition: A build automation and project management tool for Java. Manages dependencies, standardises the build lifecycle, and orchestrates plugins for compilation, testing, packaging, and deployment.
terms.memory_hook: Maven = build tool + dependency manager. The standard in the Jakarta EE world.

terms.definition: Marks a class, method, or field as obsolete. The compiler emits a warning at every call site, signalling to developers that they should migrate away.
terms.memory_hook: @Deprecated = 'this still works but please stop using it'. Compiler warns callers.

terms.definition: Tells the compiler this method overrides a method from its superclass or interface. The compiler throws an error if no matching method exists — catching typos like 'toSttring()' at compile time.
terms.memory_hook: @Override = safety net. Catches override typos before they become runtime bugs.

terms.definition: A meta-annotation controlling how long an annotation is retained. Three policies: SOURCE (discarded by compiler), CLASS (in .class file, not runtime-accessible), RUNTIME (accessible via reflection at runtime).
terms.memory_hook: @Retention(RUNTIME) = annotation survives to execution. Required for Jakarta EE processing.

terms.definition: Suppresses specific compiler warnings for the annotated element. e.g. @SuppressWarnings('unchecked') silences generic-cast warnings.
terms.memory_hook: @SuppressWarnings = mute the compiler for a known, intentional warning.

terms.definition: A meta-annotation that restricts which code elements an annotation may be applied to. Takes one or more ElementType values: TYPE, METHOD, FIELD, CONSTRUCTOR, PARAMETER, LOCAL_VARIABLE, ANNOTATION_TYPE, PACKAGE.
terms.memory_hook: @Target = 'where is this annotation allowed?' Omit = allowed everywhere.

terms.definition: A Java enum used with @Target to specify valid annotation placement targets: TYPE, METHOD, FIELD, CONSTRUCTOR, PARAMETER, LOCAL_VARIABLE, ANNOTATION_TYPE, PACKAGE.
terms.memory_hook: ElementType = the menu of places an annotation can appear.

terms.definition: The Java keyword for declaring a custom annotation type. Elements (methods) inside define the annotation's attributes, with optional default values.
terms.memory_hook: @interface = how you define a new annotation. Methods inside = annotation attributes.

terms.definition: Default retention if @Retention is omitted. The annotation is stored in the .class file but the JVM does not expose it via reflection at runtime.
terms.memory_hook: CLASS = in the bytecode, invisible at runtime. The silent default.

terms.definition: The annotation is stored in the .class file and accessible via the Java Reflection API at runtime. Required for all annotations that Jakarta EE containers process.
terms.memory_hook: RUNTIME = the annotation survives into execution. Frameworks read it with reflection.

terms.definition: The annotation exists only in source code. The compiler discards it before producing the .class file. Useful for compile-time tools like Lombok.
terms.memory_hook: SOURCE = gone after compilation. Only for source-level tools.

terms.definition: Attribute set on the ServletContext. Lives for the entire lifetime of the application and is shared across all servlets, sessions, and users.
terms.memory_hook: Application scope = global. One copy, shared by everyone, lives as long as the app.

terms.definition: A servlet mode where the container thread is released after calling request.startAsync(), while processing continues on another thread. Improves throughput for slow I/O or long computations.
terms.memory_hook: Async servlet = release the container thread immediately; finish on a background thread.

terms.definition: Servlet 3.1+ feature. Reading the request body and writing the response body do not block the executing thread. Uses ReadListener and WriteListener event listeners.
terms.memory_hook: Non-blocking I/O = handle huge uploads/downloads without occupying a thread while waiting.

terms.definition: Attribute set on the ServletRequest. Lives only for the duration of a single HTTP request. Shared between servlets and JSPs chained within the same request.
terms.memory_hook: Request scope = born with the request, dies with the response.

terms.definition: Five stages managed by the Web Container: 1-Loading, 2-Instantiation (ONE instance created), 3-Initialization (init()), 4-Service (service()/doGet() per request), 5-Destruction (destroy()).
terms.memory_hook: Load → New → init() → service() [per request] → destroy(). Only one instance is ever created.

terms.definition: Attribute set on the HttpSession. Persists across multiple requests from the same client (identified by a session cookie). Lives until timeout or explicit invalidation.
terms.memory_hook: Session scope = per-user, survives many requests. Backed by JSESSIONID cookie.

terms.definition: A Java class that runs inside the Web Container, receives HTTP requests, and generates dynamic responses. Every Jakarta EE web framework (JSF, Spring MVC) is built on at least one servlet.
terms.memory_hook: Servlet = the fundamental HTTP handler in Java. Every web framework sits on top of it.

terms.definition: Implements jakarta.servlet.Filter. Placed in the request/response pipeline before or after servlets. Used for cross-cutting concerns: authentication, logging, GZIP compression, character encoding.
terms.memory_hook: Filter = middleware for servlets. Intercepts every matching request before the servlet sees it.

terms.definition: Registers a Filter and maps it to URL patterns or servlet names. Alternative to <filter> declarations in web.xml.
terms.memory_hook: @WebFilter('/api/*') = apply this filter to all /api/* requests.

terms.definition: Registers a servlet class and maps it to URL pattern(s). The recommended replacement for servlet declarations in web.xml.
terms.memory_hook: @WebServlet('/hello') = map this servlet to /hello. No web.xml needed.

terms.definition: Override in HttpServlet to handle HTTP GET requests. Must be idempotent — reading and retrieving only, no side effects.
terms.memory_hook: doGet() = read-only requests. Must not change state.

terms.definition: Override in HttpServlet to handle HTTP POST requests. Used for form submissions, creating resources, or any operation that changes server state.
terms.memory_hook: doPost() = form submits and state-changing operations. Has a request body.

terms.definition: Passed to Filter.doFilter(). Calling chain.doFilter(request, response) passes control to the next filter or the target servlet. Not calling it blocks the request.
terms.memory_hook: chain.doFilter() = pass it on. Skip the call = block the request.

terms.definition: The abstract base class for HTTP-protocol servlets. Extend it and override doGet(), doPost(), doPut(), doDelete(), etc. to handle the corresponding HTTP verbs.
terms.memory_hook: Extend HttpServlet → override doGet/doPost. Container calls the right method per HTTP verb.

terms.definition: Represents the incoming HTTP request. Provides access to URL parameters (getParameter()), headers, request body, HTTP method, session, and path information.
terms.memory_hook: HttpServletRequest = the incoming request. Everything about the client's call is here.

terms.definition: Represents the outgoing HTTP response. Use setStatus() for the status code, setHeader() for headers, getWriter() or getOutputStream() to write the body.
terms.memory_hook: HttpServletResponse = the outgoing response. Write your output and status here.

terms.definition: The standard web application deployment descriptor (WEB-INF/web.xml). Declares servlets, filters, listeners, security constraints, welcome files, and session config. Optional when using annotations.
terms.memory_hook: web.xml = the XML fallback for things annotations can't or shouldn't do.

terms.definition: Contexts and Dependency Injection. Jakarta EE's core programming model. Provides type-safe dependency injection, contextual bean scopes, interceptors, decorators, and a powerful event system. Replaces EJB for most use cases.
terms.memory_hook: CDI = the backbone of modern Jakarta EE. Every major component is a CDI bean.

terms.definition: A bean that replaces another bean at deployment time, activated in beans.xml. Useful for test doubles or environment-specific implementations.
terms.memory_hook: Alternative = swap-in a replacement bean per deployment. Activated in beans.xml.

terms.definition: Implements the Decorator design pattern for CDI beans. Implements the same interface as the decorated bean and adds or overrides specific business methods.
terms.memory_hook: Decorator = wraps a bean's business logic. Unlike interceptor, understands the business interface.

terms.definition: A type-safe observer/event system. An emitter injects Event<T> and calls event.fire(payload). Any bean with an @Observes T method is notified synchronously. No compile-time coupling between emitter and observers.
terms.memory_hook: CDI Event = in-process pub/sub with zero coupling. Fire → all observers are called.

terms.definition: Intercepts method calls on CDI-managed beans to apply cross-cutting behaviour (transactions, security, logging) without modifying the bean's code.
terms.memory_hook: Interceptor = wrap a method call with extra behaviour. Reusable, declarative decorator.

terms.definition: A design pattern where an object's dependencies are supplied by an external mechanism (the CDI container) rather than created with 'new'. Decouples components and makes code testable.
terms.memory_hook: DI = 'I need a thing' → the container hands it to you. No 'new' needed.

terms.definition: A broad design principle: the framework calls your code, not vice versa. Dependency Injection is the most common form of IoC in Jakarta EE.
terms.memory_hook: IoC = Hollywood principle: 'Don't call us, we'll call you.' Container is in charge.

terms.definition: Any plain Java class that CDI can discover, instantiate, and manage. No special interface or superclass is required.
terms.memory_hook: Managed Bean = any ordinary Java class CDI knows about. No interface needed.

terms.definition: A custom annotation (meta-annotated with @Qualifier and @Retention(RUNTIME)) that disambiguates between multiple beans of the same type. Both the bean class and the injection point carry the same qualifier.
terms.memory_hook: Qualifier = a label saying 'specifically THIS bean'. Resolves ambiguous injection.

terms.definition: One bean instance for the entire application. Shared by all users, sessions, and requests. Thread-safe singleton.
terms.memory_hook: @ApplicationScoped = one instance for the whole app. Lives as long as the server.

terms.definition: The default pseudo-scope. A new instance is created per injection point; its lifetime is bound to the containing object. No shared state.
terms.memory_hook: @Dependent = no scope of its own. Lives and dies with whoever uses it.

terms.definition: Tells the CDI container to inject a managed bean into the annotated field, constructor parameter, or initializer method.
terms.memory_hook: @Inject = 'give me a CDI bean of this type'. Container does the wiring.

terms.definition: Placed on a method parameter to register that method as a CDI event observer. The container calls it whenever an event of the matching type is fired.
terms.memory_hook: void on(@Observes MyEvent e) {} — called automatically when MyEvent is fired.

terms.definition: Marks a void method to be called after the bean is fully instantiated and all @Inject dependencies are set. Used for initialisation logic that needs injected resources.
terms.memory_hook: @PostConstruct = 'run this after I'm fully built and all dependencies are injected'.

terms.definition: Marks a void method called just before the bean is destroyed. Used for cleanup: closing connections, releasing resources.
terms.memory_hook: @PreDestroy = 'run this cleanup code just before I'm destroyed'.

terms.definition: Declares the MIME type(s) the method returns. @Produces(MediaType.APPLICATION_JSON) serialises the return value as JSON.
terms.memory_hook: @Produces = 'I respond with this format'. Matches the client's Accept header.

terms.definition: Applied to a user-defined annotation to designate it as a CDI qualifier. The annotation must also have @Retention(RUNTIME) and appropriate @Target settings.
terms.memory_hook: Add @Qualifier to your annotation → it becomes a CDI disambiguation label.

terms.definition: One bean instance per HTTP request (or EJB/web service invocation). Created at request start, destroyed when the response is sent.
terms.memory_hook: @RequestScoped = born and dies with one HTTP request. The most common scope.

terms.definition: One bean instance per user HTTP session. Created when the session starts, destroyed when it expires. Must implement Serializable.
terms.memory_hook: @SessionScoped = per logged-in user. Shopping cart, user preferences.

terms.definition: A CDI interceptor binding that wraps the annotated method/class in a JTA transaction. The container begins the transaction before the method and commits on success or rolls back on unchecked exception.
terms.memory_hook: @Transactional = 'wrap this in a DB transaction'. No UserTransaction boilerplate.

terms.definition: One bean instance per active JTA transaction. Created when a transaction begins, destroyed on commit or rollback.
terms.memory_hook: @TransactionScoped = lives exactly as long as one database transaction.

terms.definition: The CDI interface injected to fire events of type T. fire(T payload) notifies all matching @Observes methods synchronously. fireAsync() notifies asynchronously.
terms.memory_hook: @Inject Event<OrderPlaced> event; event.fire(new OrderPlaced()); → all observers are called.

terms.definition: An optional CDI configuration file (META-INF/beans.xml or WEB-INF/beans.xml). Configures alternatives, interceptor/decorator order, or sets the bean-discovery-mode. CDI works without it via auto-discovery.
terms.memory_hook: beans.xml = optional CDI tuning file. Auto-discovery works perfectly without it.

terms.definition: A component model for server-side business logic. EJBs are CDI beans with additional enterprise services: transaction management, pooling, concurrency control, and optional remote access.
terms.memory_hook: EJB = CDI bean with extra enterprise powers. Transactions, pooling, and security built in.

terms.definition: Opt-in model where the developer manually manages transactions using UserTransaction. Gives full control but requires more code.
terms.memory_hook: BMT = you write begin/commit/rollback yourself with UserTransaction. Full control, more work.

terms.definition: The default transaction model for EJBs. The container begins, commits, or rolls back transactions automatically based on @TransactionAttribute settings. No UserTransaction code needed.
terms.memory_hook: CMT = container handles transactions. You just annotate; container does begin/commit/rollback.

terms.definition: EJBs can schedule method execution with @Schedule (cron-like syntax) or the TimerService API. Used for scheduled tasks like nightly cleanups or reports.
terms.memory_hook: @Schedule(hour='2', minute='0') = run this method at 2:00 AM daily. Cron for Java.

terms.definition: An EJB that consumes JMS messages asynchronously. Implements jakarta.jms.MessageListener. The container calls onMessage() and manages its lifecycle and transaction.
terms.memory_hook: MDB = JMS consumer activated by the container. No polling — container pushes the message.

terms.definition: Marks an EJB business interface as local — callable only from within the same JVM. Parameters passed by reference. More efficient than @Remote.
terms.memory_hook: @Local = same JVM only. No serialisation. Prefer this whenever possible.

terms.definition: No-interface local view. The EJB class itself is the business interface. No separate interface needed. The simplest EJB style.
terms.memory_hook: @LocalBean = skip the interface. The class is directly callable. Most modern EJBs use this.

terms.definition: Marks a Message-Driven Bean (MDB). Activated by the container when a JMS message arrives. Implements MessageListener.onMessage(). Fully asynchronous.
terms.memory_hook: @MessageDriven = 'wake me up when a JMS message arrives'. The async consumer EJB.

terms.definition: Marks an EJB business interface as remote — callable from a different JVM via RMI/IIOP. Parameters are serialised.
terms.memory_hook: @Remote = callable from another JVM. Parameters are serialised. Slower than @Local.

terms.definition: Exactly one instance per application, shared by all concurrent callers. Supports concurrency control via @Lock. Good for application-level caches or startup initialisation.
terms.memory_hook: @Singleton = one instance, app-wide. Use @Lock to control concurrent access.

terms.definition: Marks a Stateful Session Bean. Maintains conversational state with a specific client across multiple method calls. One dedicated instance per client. Destroyed by @Remove.
terms.memory_hook: @Stateful = remembers its client across calls. Use for multi-step workflows like checkout.

terms.definition: Marks a Stateless Session Bean. No per-client state between calls. The container pools instances and assigns one per request.
terms.memory_hook: @Stateless = pooled and stateless. Fast and scalable. Best for request-scoped logic.

terms.definition: Controls EJB transaction behaviour. Key values: REQUIRED (join existing or start new — default), REQUIRES_NEW (always start fresh), MANDATORY (existing transaction required), NEVER (must not be in a transaction).
terms.memory_hook: REQUIRED = join or create. REQUIRES_NEW = always fresh. MANDATORY = must already exist.

terms.definition: Jakarta XML Web Services. Optional Jakarta EE API for building SOAP-based web service endpoints and clients. Not in Core or Web Profile by default. Can expose Java classes or EJBs as SOAP services.
terms.memory_hook: JAX-WS = SOAP in Java. The older, more formal alternative to JAX-RS.

terms.definition: Handles two-way mapping between Java objects and XML. JAX-WS relies on JAXB to marshal Java parameters into SOAP XML and unmarshal incoming XML back to Java objects.
terms.memory_hook: JAXB = JAX-WS's serialisation layer. Java ↔ XML. The XML equivalent of JSON-B.

terms.definition: Code-first SOAP strategy. Write and annotate the Java class first; the framework generates the WSDL. Simplest when exposing an existing Java class.
terms.memory_hook: Bottom-up = code first, WSDL is generated. The easiest path.

terms.definition: Simple Object Access Protocol. A platform-independent XML-based messaging protocol for structured communication between distributed applications. Uses an XML envelope with optional header and body. Typically runs over HTTP.
terms.memory_hook: SOAP = XML in a formal envelope. Verbose but widely supported in enterprise systems.

terms.definition: Contract-first SOAP strategy. Write the WSDL first to define the service contract; then generate Java skeleton code from it. Best for strict multi-platform interoperability.
terms.memory_hook: Top-down = WSDL first, Java code generated. Choose when strict contracts are required.

terms.definition: Specifies the concrete protocol (e.g. SOAP 1.1 over HTTP) and data format for each portType operation. Bridges the abstract interface with a specific transport.
terms.memory_hook: binding = portType + protocol. Maps abstract operations to SOAP/HTTP.

terms.definition: The abstract interface section of a WSDL. Defines the operations the service supports and, for each operation, the input, output, and fault messages.
terms.memory_hook: portType = 'what can this service do?' — like a Java interface in WSDL.

terms.definition: The concrete endpoint section of a WSDL. Specifies the network address (URL) where the bound service is reachable.
terms.memory_hook: service = the actual URL to call. The most concrete part of WSDL.

terms.definition: Marks a method as a SOAP web service operation to be included in the generated WSDL. Methods without this annotation are not exposed.
terms.memory_hook: @WebMethod = 'include this in the WSDL'. Unlisted methods stay private.

terms.definition: Marks a Java class or interface as a SOAP web service endpoint. The runtime generates a WSDL document from the annotated class.
terms.memory_hook: @WebService = expose this class as a SOAP endpoint. WSDL is auto-generated.

terms.definition: Web Service Description Language. An XML document formally describing a SOAP service: operations (portType), message formats (types, message), protocol binding, and the network address (service/port).
terms.memory_hook: WSDL = the complete contract for a SOAP service. Clients use it to generate stub code.

terms.definition: A standard API for declaring and executing validation constraints on Java objects using annotations or XML. Integrates with JPA (pre-persist), JSF (form submit), and JAX-RS (@Valid parameter validation).
terms.memory_hook: Bean Validation = annotate once, validated everywhere. JPA, JSF, JAX-RS all honour it.

terms.definition: Validation triggered automatically by the container without the developer calling Validator: JPA before persist/update, JSF on form submit, JAX-RS with @Valid.
terms.memory_hook: Implicit = fires automatically. Explicit = you call Validator.validate() yourself.

terms.definition: Meta-annotation on a custom constraint annotation. The validatedBy attribute names the ConstraintValidator class containing the validation logic.
terms.memory_hook: @Constraint(validatedBy=MyValidator.class) = link your annotation to its implementation.

terms.definition: Constraint: the annotated number must be ≤ value.
terms.memory_hook: @Max(100) = must not exceed 100.

terms.definition: Constraint: the annotated number must be ≥ value.
terms.memory_hook: @Min(1) = must be at least 1.

terms.definition: Constraint: the annotated element must not be null.
terms.memory_hook: @NotNull = must exist. Simple null check.

terms.definition: Constraint: the element's size must be within [min, max]. Applies to String (chars), Collection, Map, and array.
terms.memory_hook: @Size(max=200) = up to 200 characters or elements.

terms.definition: Triggers cascaded Bean Validation on a method parameter, return value, or field. In JAX-RS, a validation failure automatically returns HTTP 400 Bad Request.
terms.memory_hook: @Valid on a param = 'validate this whole object'. HTTP 400 on failure in JAX-RS.

terms.definition: Implements the logic for a custom constraint. Type params: A = the annotation type, T = the type being validated. isValid() returns true if the value satisfies the constraint.
terms.memory_hook: ConstraintValidator = where you write the actual validation logic. isValid() = the check.

terms.definition: Describes one validation failure: which constraint was violated, the invalid value, the property path, and the error message.
terms.memory_hook: ConstraintViolation = one failed rule. getMessage() gives the human-readable error.

terms.definition: jakarta.validation.Validator. Programmatic entry point for Bean Validation. validate(bean) returns a Set<ConstraintViolation<T>> — empty = all passed, non-empty = violations found.
terms.memory_hook: Validator.validate(bean) → empty Set = clean. Non-empty = violations.

terms.definition: Jakarta ServerFaces. A component-based web UI framework following MVC. Pages are built from reusable component trees backed by managed beans, described in Facelets XHTML.
terms.memory_hook: JSF = Java's server-side MVC web framework. XHTML view + CDI bean + FacesServlet.

terms.definition: Partial page updates without a full reload, enabled by the <f:ajax> tag. Allows submitting a form or refreshing a panel asynchronously.
terms.memory_hook: <f:ajax> = partial update without full page reload.

terms.definition: An action method returns a String outcome that names the target page directly (e.g. return 'dashboard' → dashboard.xhtml). No configuration needed.
terms.memory_hook: return 'success' → navigates to success.xhtml. Simple and direct.

terms.definition: A compact language used in Facelets XHTML via #{...} syntax. Binds component values to bean properties (two-way for input fields) and wires action attributes to bean methods.
terms.memory_hook: #{bean.property} = read/write bean field. #{bean.method()} = call bean action.

terms.definition: The standard JSF view technology (since JSF 2.0). Uses XHTML files to declare component trees, supports powerful templating (<ui:template>, <ui:define>), and provides better error messages than JSP.
terms.memory_hook: Facelets = XHTML view files. Declare your JSF UI here. Better than JSP in every way.

terms.definition: Navigation rules in faces-config.xml using <navigation-rule> elements. Maps action outcome strings to target view IDs. Useful for complex conditional navigation.
terms.memory_hook: faces-config.xml <navigation-rule> = 'if outcome is X, go to page Y'.

terms.definition: Six phases run for every Faces request: 1-Restore View, 2-Apply Request Values, 3-Process Validations, 4-Update Model Values, 5-Invoke Application, 6-Render Response. Validation errors skip directly to Render Response.
terms.memory_hook: 6 phases: Restore → Apply → Validate → Update → Invoke → Render. Errors skip to Render.

terms.definition: The pattern underpinning JSF: Model = CDI managed beans (data + logic), View = Facelets XHTML pages (UI), Controller = FacesServlet (processes requests, runs lifecycle).
terms.memory_hook: JSF MVC: Bean=Model, .xhtml=View, FacesServlet=Controller.

terms.definition: HTML5 alignment features. Pass-through attributes (pt:placeholder) add native HTML5 attributes to JSF components. Pass-through elements let plain HTML5 tags be treated as JSF components.
terms.memory_hook: Pass-through = HTML5 and JSF coexist in the same page.

terms.definition: The single front controller servlet for all JSF requests. Drives the 6-phase lifecycle for every Faces request and produces the rendered HTML response.
terms.memory_hook: FacesServlet = the one servlet that ALL JSF requests pass through.

terms.definition: A CDI bean annotated with @Named (and a scope) that backs a JSF view. Exposes properties (data) and action methods (event handlers) bound via EL expressions.
terms.memory_hook: @Named @SessionScoped class CartBean = a JSF backing bean. Bound via #{cartBean}.

terms.definition: Shorthand stereotype = @Named + @RequestScoped. Marks a CDI bean as a request-scoped JSF model bean.
terms.memory_hook: @Model = @Named + @RequestScoped in one annotation.

terms.definition: Makes a CDI bean accessible by name in EL expressions. Default name = class name with first letter lowercased (CartBean → #{cartBean}).
terms.memory_hook: @Named = 'expose this bean in the view as #{beanName}'.

terms.definition: The Jakarta EE standard for ORM. Defines how Java objects map to relational database tables via annotations. Provides a high-level persistence API on top of JDBC.
terms.memory_hook: JPA = map Java classes to database tables without writing SQL.

terms.definition: Type-safe, programmatic JPA query building in Java code. No string literals — the Metamodel provides compile-time-safe field references.
terms.memory_hook: Criteria API = build JPA queries with Java code. Compile-time type safety.

terms.definition: Related entities are loaded immediately in the same query as the parent. Default for @ManyToOne and @OneToOne. Risk: loading too much data when not needed.
terms.memory_hook: Eager = load everything now. Can cause N+1 problems if overused.

terms.definition: Related entities/collections are NOT loaded until first accessed. Default for @OneToMany and @ManyToMany. Accessing outside an active transaction causes LazyInitializationException.
terms.memory_hook: Lazy = load on demand. Danger: access outside a transaction = exception.

terms.definition: Translating between Java objects and relational database tables/rows. JPA annotations describe the mapping; the JPA provider (Hibernate, EclipseLink) generates and executes the SQL.
terms.memory_hook: ORM = Java ↔ SQL translator. You work with objects; JPA writes the SQL.

terms.definition: A first-level cache of managed entity instances tracked by the EntityManager. Dirty checking detects field changes and flushes them to the database at transaction commit.
terms.memory_hook: Persistence Context = entity working set. Change a managed entity → JPA writes it on commit.

terms.definition: A named logical grouping in persistence.xml that associates entity classes with a datasource and JPA provider config. Referenced by @PersistenceContext(unitName='myPU').
terms.memory_hook: Persistence Unit = 'these entities use this database connection'.

terms.definition: Customises the mapped column: name, length, nullable, unique, insertable, updatable.
terms.memory_hook: @Column(name='FIRST_NAME', length=100, nullable=false) = full column control.

terms.definition: Marks a Java class as a JPA entity — instances are persisted as database rows. Requires a no-arg constructor and an @Id field.
terms.memory_hook: @Entity = 'store objects of this class as rows in the database'.

terms.definition: Specifies how the PK is auto-generated. Strategies: AUTO, IDENTITY (db auto-increment), SEQUENCE, TABLE.
terms.memory_hook: @GeneratedValue = the database generates this ID. Don't set it manually.

terms.definition: Marks the field that is the entity's primary key. Every @Entity must have exactly one @Id.
terms.memory_hook: @Id = this field is the primary key. Mandatory on every @Entity.

terms.definition: Maps a many-to-many relationship, stored in a join table. Default fetch: LAZY.
terms.memory_hook: @ManyToMany = students ↔ courses. Requires a join table.

terms.definition: Maps a many-to-one relationship. Placed on the FK field on the 'many' side. Default fetch: EAGER.
terms.memory_hook: @ManyToOne = 'I have a single reference to my parent'. Default: EAGER. The child side.

terms.definition: Maps a one-to-many relationship. Placed on the collection field on the 'one' side. Default fetch: LAZY.
terms.memory_hook: @OneToMany = 'I own a list of the other entity'. Default: LAZY. The parent side.

terms.definition: Maps an exclusive one-to-one relationship between two entities.
terms.memory_hook: @OneToOne = exclusive pairing between two entity types.

terms.definition: Injects a container-managed EntityManager. unitName links to a persistence unit in persistence.xml.
terms.memory_hook: @PersistenceContext EntityManager em = the only way to get an EM in Jakarta EE.

terms.definition: Customises the mapped database table (name, schema, unique constraints). Optional — defaults to the class name.
terms.memory_hook: @Table(name='ORDERS') = override the default table name.

terms.definition: The central JPA interface. Provides the full CRUD API: persist(), find(), merge(), remove(), and methods to create JPQL/Criteria/native SQL queries.
terms.memory_hook: EntityManager = the JPA Swiss army knife. persist/find/merge/remove + queries.

terms.definition: Heavyweight factory that creates EntityManager instances for one Persistence Unit. Created once per application.
terms.memory_hook: EntityManagerFactory creates EntityManagers. Expensive to create — make it once.

terms.definition: Java Persistence Query Language. Object-oriented SQL-like language that operates on entity class names and field names, not database table/column names.
terms.memory_hook: JPQL = SQL but for Java objects. 'FROM Book b' not 'FROM BOOK'.

terms.definition: JPA configuration file at META-INF/persistence.xml. Defines persistence units: datasource JNDI name, transaction type (JTA or RESOURCE_LOCAL), entity classes, and provider properties.
terms.memory_hook: persistence.xml = JPA's setup file. Without it, JPA doesn't know which database to use.

terms.definition: The JPA reference implementation, bundled with GlassFish and Payara.
terms.memory_hook: EclipseLink = the official JPA reference provider. Default on GlassFish/Payara.

terms.definition: The most widely used JPA persistence provider. Implements the full JPA spec plus many Hibernate-specific extensions.
terms.memory_hook: Hibernate = the JPA provider you're most likely using. The de-facto JPA standard.

terms.definition: Jakarta RESTful Web Services. Standard Jakarta EE API for building REST services and clients using annotations. Part of both the Web and Core profiles.
terms.memory_hook: JAX-RS = REST in Java. @Path + HTTP verb annotations = a REST endpoint.

terms.definition: HTTP mechanism where client and server agree on response format via Accept (client) and @Produces (server) headers.
terms.memory_hook: Content negotiation = Accept header (client) ↔ @Produces (server). They must match.

terms.definition: Representational State Transfer. Stateless, resource-oriented architectural style over HTTP. Resources identified by URIs, operations mapped to HTTP verbs (GET/POST/PUT/DELETE), data in JSON or XML.
terms.memory_hook: REST = resources + HTTP verbs + stateless. The dominant web API style.

terms.definition: Unidirectional server-to-client real-time streaming over a single HTTP connection. Server pushes events whenever data is available. JAX-RS implements it via SseEventSink and Sse.
terms.memory_hook: SSE = server → client push. One HTTP connection, server pushes at will. One direction only.

terms.definition: Placed on a subclass of Application. Defines the base URI for all JAX-RS resources. @ApplicationPath('api') → all resources start with /api/.
terms.memory_hook: @ApplicationPath('api') = every REST endpoint starts with /api/.

terms.definition: Aggregates multiple JAX-RS parameter annotations (@QueryParam, @PathParam, etc.) into a single POJO. Keeps resource method signatures clean.
terms.memory_hook: @BeanParam = bundle all request params into one object.

terms.definition: Declares the MIME type(s) the method accepts in the request body. @Consumes(MediaType.APPLICATION_JSON) expects a JSON body.
terms.memory_hook: @Consumes = 'I expect this format in the request'. Matches Content-Type header.

terms.definition: Map a Java method to the HTTP verb. CRUD: @GET=Read, @POST=Create, @PUT=Full update, @DELETE=Remove, @PATCH=Partial update.
terms.memory_hook: Verb annotations = CRUD. GET→Read, POST→Create, PUT→Update, DELETE→Delete.

terms.definition: Defines the URI path for a resource class or method. Supports template variables: @Path('orders/{id}'). Class-level = base path; method-level = sub-path.
terms.memory_hook: @Path('users/{id}') = this method handles /api/users/42.

terms.definition: Injects a URI path template variable into a method parameter. /users/{userId} + @PathParam('userId') → userId=42.
terms.memory_hook: @PathParam = grab the {variable} from the URL path.

terms.definition: Declares the MIME type(s) the method returns. @Produces(MediaType.APPLICATION_JSON) serialises the return value as JSON.
terms.memory_hook: @Produces = 'I respond with this format'. Matches the client's Accept header.

terms.definition: Registers a JAX-RS extension (ExceptionMapper, MessageBodyReader/Writer, filter, etc.) with the runtime automatically.
terms.memory_hook: @Provider = 'auto-register this as a JAX-RS plug-in'.

terms.definition: Injects a URL query parameter value. ?page=2 → @QueryParam('page') int page.
terms.memory_hook: @QueryParam = grab a ?key=value from the query string.

terms.definition: Subclass of jakarta.ws.rs.core.Application annotated with @ApplicationPath. The JAX-RS configuration root for the application.
terms.memory_hook: extends Application + @ApplicationPath = the JAX-RS bootstrap class.

terms.definition: Maps a specific exception type to an HTTP response. Implement toResponse(T exception) and annotate with @Provider.
terms.memory_hook: ExceptionMapper = catch a Java exception → return a clean HTTP error response.

terms.definition: jakarta.ws.rs.core.Response. Builder class for a complete HTTP response with specific status, headers, and entity body. e.g. Response.ok(entity).build(), Response.status(404).build().
terms.memory_hook: Response = full control over the HTTP response. Use when you need custom status or headers.

terms.definition: Returns HTTP 201 Created with Location header set to the URI of the new resource. The standard JAX-RS response after a successful POST.
terms.memory_hook: Response.created(uri).entity(body).build() = correct response after create.

terms.definition: Core interface of the JAX-RS client API. Represents a web resource URI. Chain .path(), .queryParam(), then call .request().get()/.post() etc.
terms.memory_hook: WebTarget = fluent URI builder for JAX-RS client calls.

terms.definition: Higher-level API that automatically maps Java objects to/from JSON. Handles serialisation (toJson) and deserialisation (fromJson) without manual parsing.
terms.memory_hook: JSON-B = auto-mapping Java objects ↔ JSON. Simpler than JSON-P for POJO use.

terms.definition: Low-level standard API for parsing, generating, transforming, and querying JSON. Two approaches: Object Model API (in-memory tree) and Streaming API (token-by-token, low memory).
terms.memory_hook: JSON-P = low-level JSON toolkit. Build trees or stream tokens.

terms.definition: Reads the entire JSON document into a tree of JsonObject/JsonArray nodes. Allows random access. Use Json.createObjectBuilder() to write, Json.createReader() to read.
terms.memory_hook: Object Model = full JSON tree in RAM. Easy random access. Use for small-to-medium docs.

terms.definition: Processes JSON as a stream of tokens without loading the full document into memory. Use Json.createGenerator() to write, Json.createParser() to read.
terms.memory_hook: Streaming = one token at a time. Low memory, forward-only. Use for large JSON files.

terms.definition: Specifies the date/time format pattern for a date field. @JsonbDateFormat('dd-MM-yyyy') outputs dates as '25-12-2024'.
terms.memory_hook: @JsonbDateFormat = control how dates appear in JSON.

terms.definition: Specifies a number format pattern (DecimalFormat syntax) for a numeric field in JSON-B output.
terms.memory_hook: @JsonbNumberFormat('#0.00') = control decimal precision in JSON.

terms.definition: Overrides the JSON key name for a Java field. @JsonbProperty('person-name') on field 'name' serialises it as {'person-name': 'Alice'}.
terms.memory_hook: @JsonbProperty('key') = rename this field in the JSON output.

terms.definition: Excludes a field from JSON-B processing. It will not appear in JSON output and will not be populated on input.
terms.memory_hook: @JsonbTransient = hide this field from JSON entirely.

terms.definition: Factory class entry point for all JSON-P objects: createObjectBuilder(), createArrayBuilder(), createReader(), createWriter(), createGenerator(), createParser(), createPointer(), createPatch().
terms.memory_hook: Json.create___() = start every JSON-P operation here.

terms.definition: Main JSON-B interface. Created via JsonbBuilder.create(). toJson(Object) → String, fromJson(String, Class<T>) → T.
terms.memory_hook: Jsonb jsonb = JsonbBuilder.create(); jsonb.toJson(obj); jsonb.fromJson(str, MyClass.class);

terms.definition: Interface for defining custom type-to-JSON mapping. Implement JsonbAdapter<OriginalType, AdaptedType> and register via JsonbConfig.withAdapters().
terms.memory_hook: JsonbAdapter = custom converter for types JSON-B can't map automatically.

terms.definition: Factory class for creating Jsonb instances. JsonbBuilder.create() for default config; JsonbBuilder.create(config) for custom.
terms.memory_hook: JsonbBuilder = the Jsonb factory. Always start here.

terms.definition: Configures JSON-B: property ordering, null handling, custom serialisers/adapters, date formats.
terms.memory_hook: JsonbConfig = customise JSON-B serialisation/deserialisation behaviour.

terms.definition: Applies a sequence of RFC 6902 operations (add, remove, replace, move, copy, test) to a JSON document to produce a modified document.
terms.memory_hook: JsonPatch = surgical edits to a JSON document. Like git diff applied to JSON.

terms.definition: References a specific location in a JSON document using RFC 6901 syntax (e.g. '/address/city'). Used to read, add, remove, or replace a value.
terms.memory_hook: JsonPointer('/address/city') = a path to one value in a JSON document.

terms.definition: Standard Java API for asynchronous message exchange via a messaging broker. Producers send to destinations; consumers receive at their own pace. Decouples producers from consumers in time and location.
terms.memory_hook: JMS = async messaging. Producer and consumer never need to be online at the same time.

terms.definition: Defines how a consumer confirms message receipt. AUTO_ACKNOWLEDGE: auto-ACKed after onMessage() returns. CLIENT_ACKNOWLEDGE: consumer calls message.acknowledge(). DUPS_OK_ACKNOWLEDGE: lazy, duplicates possible.
terms.memory_hook: AUTO_ACKNOWLEDGE = safe default. Message ACKed when onMessage() returns normally.

terms.definition: A SQL-92 filter expression on message headers/properties. Only messages matching the expression are delivered to the subscribing consumer.
terms.memory_hook: Message Selector = SQL WHERE clause for JMS. Filter before delivery.

terms.definition: JMS messaging model using queues. Each message is delivered to exactly one consumer, even if multiple consumers are listening on the same queue.
terms.memory_hook: P2P = queue-based. One message → exactly one consumer.

terms.definition: JMS messaging model using topics. Each published message is broadcast to all active subscribers simultaneously. Offline subscribers miss messages unless durable subscriptions are used.
terms.memory_hook: Pub/Sub = topic-based. One message → all subscribers.

terms.definition: JMS message carrying raw binary data (byte array).
terms.memory_hook: BytesMessage = binary data in JMS.

terms.definition: An administered JMS object, looked up from JNDI, that creates JMS Connection objects to the messaging provider.
terms.memory_hook: Connection Factory = JNDI-registered factory for JMS broker connections.

terms.definition: A JMS client that receives and processes messages from a destination. Can be synchronous (receive()) or asynchronous (MessageListener/MDB).
terms.memory_hook: Consumer = the JMS receiver.

terms.definition: A JMS client that creates and sends messages to a destination (queue or topic).
terms.memory_hook: Producer = the JMS sender.

terms.definition: JMS message carrying a serialized Java object. The object must implement Serializable.
terms.memory_hook: ObjectMessage = Serializable Java object in a JMS envelope.

terms.definition: JMS Point-to-Point destination. Messages persist until consumed. Each message is consumed exactly once.
terms.memory_hook: Queue = FIFO mailbox. One consumer gets each message.

terms.definition: JMS message whose body is a String. The most commonly used message type.
terms.memory_hook: TextMessage = String in a JMS envelope.

terms.definition: JMS Publish/Subscribe destination. Published messages are broadcast to all active subscribers.
terms.memory_hook: Topic = broadcast channel. Every subscriber gets a copy.

terms.definition: Inside @MessageDriven, configures the activation spec: destinationLookup (JNDI name) and destinationType (Queue or Topic).
terms.memory_hook: @ActivationConfigProperty = wire an MDB to a specific queue or topic by JNDI name.

terms.definition: Declares a JMS destination as an application resource. The server creates the queue/topic at deployment if it does not already exist.
terms.memory_hook: @JMSDestinationDefinition = 'create this queue if it doesn't exist'.

terms.definition: Simplified JMS 2.0 API combining Connection and Session. Injected via @Inject in Jakarta EE. Use createProducer().send(destination, body) to send messages.
terms.memory_hook: @Inject JMSContext ctx; ctx.createProducer().send(queue, 'hello'); — the modern JMS API.

terms.definition: jakarta.jms.MessageListener interface. Defines onMessage(Message). An MDB implements this; the container calls it when a message is available.
terms.memory_hook: MessageListener.onMessage() = the callback the container invokes to deliver a JMS message.

terms.definition: The open-source JMS message broker bundled with GlassFish and Payara. The default messaging provider for those servers.
terms.memory_hook: OpenMQ = the JMS broker bundled with GlassFish/Payara.

terms.definition: Jakarta EE standard for batch processing — developing and running jobs that process large volumes of data, typically offline or on a schedule.
terms.memory_hook: Jakarta Batch = bulk data processing. Process millions of records offline.

terms.definition: A step that executes a single atomic task once, runs to completion, and returns an exit status String. Implements the Batchlet interface. Used for non-item-oriented tasks.
terms.memory_hook: Batchlet = run once, finish, return a status. Like a script doing one thing.

terms.definition: A savepoint recorded by the batch runtime after each chunk commit. On job failure, processing can restart from the last checkpoint rather than the beginning.
terms.memory_hook: Checkpoint = auto-save for batch jobs. Fail and restart from here, not from scratch.

terms.definition: A step that loops: read N items (ItemReader), process each (ItemProcessor), write the chunk (ItemWriter). A checkpoint is saved after each chunk commit. Commit interval is configurable.
terms.memory_hook: Chunk = read → process → write, in repeating batches. The core batch pattern.

terms.definition: JSL element for conditional flow control between steps based on exit status. Implements the Decider interface.
terms.memory_hook: Decision = if/else branching in the batch job flow.

terms.definition: JSL element that groups a sequence of steps into a named unit. Flows can run in parallel via the split element.
terms.memory_hook: Flow = a named group of steps. Put steps inside flows to organise or parallelise them.

terms.definition: Top-level batch unit. A logical collection of steps encapsulating one complete batch process, defined in a JSL file.
terms.memory_hook: Job = the whole batch process from start to finish.

terms.definition: Stores persistent state of all batch job executions: execution IDs, step results, status (STARTED, COMPLETED, FAILED), and timestamps.
terms.memory_hook: JobRepository = the history of every batch job run.

terms.definition: JSL element enclosing multiple flows that execute in parallel. Control returns to the next element only after all flows complete.
terms.memory_hook: Split = parallel execution. Multiple flows run at the same time.

terms.definition: An independent, sequential unit of work in a job. Either a Chunk step (item-oriented) or a Batchlet step (task-oriented).
terms.memory_hook: Step = one task in the batch job.

terms.definition: Bootstrap class for the Jakarta Batch API. BatchRuntime.getJobOperator() is the single entry point.
terms.memory_hook: BatchRuntime.getJobOperator() = always the first call to control batch jobs.

terms.definition: Optional CDI bean implementing Batch ItemProcessor. processItem(item) transforms or filters one item. Return null to skip.
terms.memory_hook: ItemProcessor.processItem() = transform or skip. Return null = skip this item.

terms.definition: CDI bean implementing the Batch ItemReader interface. readItem() returns one item; returns null when input is exhausted.
terms.memory_hook: ItemReader.readItem() = read one item per call. Return null to stop.

terms.definition: CDI bean implementing Batch ItemWriter. writeItems(list) receives a full chunk and writes all items to the output destination.
terms.memory_hook: ItemWriter.writeItems(list) = write a whole chunk. Good for bulk database inserts.

terms.definition: Programmatic API for controlling batch jobs. Via BatchRuntime.getJobOperator(). Key methods: start(), stop(), restart(), getJobExecution().
terms.memory_hook: JobOperator = the remote control for batch jobs. start/stop/restart.

terms.definition: XML dialect for defining batch job structure: steps, flows, splits, decisions, transitions, and properties. Files live in META-INF/batch-jobs/.
terms.memory_hook: JSL = the XML blueprint for a batch job. Defines what happens and in what order.

terms.definition: Communication where both parties send and receive simultaneously over the same connection.
terms.memory_hook: Full-duplex = a phone call. Both sides talk and listen at the same time.

terms.definition: A network protocol providing a persistent, full-duplex TCP connection. Either party can send messages at any time without waiting for the other. Ideal for real-time applications.
terms.memory_hook: WebSocket = persistent two-way pipe. Server can push to client anytime. No polling.

terms.definition: Marks a class as a programmatic Java WebSocket client endpoint.
terms.memory_hook: @ClientEndpoint = a Java class acting as a WebSocket client.

terms.definition: Marks the method called when the WebSocket connection is closed by either party.
terms.memory_hook: @OnClose = 'the connection just closed'.

terms.definition: Marks the method called when an error occurs on the WebSocket connection. Receives a Throwable.
terms.memory_hook: @OnError = 'something went wrong with this connection'.

terms.definition: Marks the method called when a text, binary, or pong message arrives.
terms.memory_hook: @OnMessage = 'a message arrived from the client'.

terms.definition: Marks the method called when a client establishes a new WebSocket connection. Receives a Session parameter.
terms.memory_hook: @OnOpen = 'a new client just connected'.

terms.definition: Marks a class as a server-side WebSocket endpoint. Value = the URI path. The container registers it and manages connections.
terms.memory_hook: @ServerEndpoint('/chat') = this class handles WebSocket connections at ws://host/chat.

terms.definition: Represents one active WebSocket connection. session.getBasicRemote().sendText(msg) pushes a message to the client. session.getAsyncRemote().sendText(msg) for non-blocking push.
terms.memory_hook: WebSocket Session = the live connection handle. sendText() to push to client.

terms.definition: Portable Jakarta EE security API covering three concerns: authentication (HttpAuthenticationMechanism), identity storage (IdentityStore), and programmatic security access (SecurityContext).
terms.memory_hook: Jakarta Security = three pillars: AuthMechanism + IdentityStore + SecurityContext.

terms.definition: Compact, self-contained, cryptographically signed token. Three Base64-encoded parts: Header (algorithm), Payload (claims: sub, groups, iss, exp), Signature. Tamper-proof.
terms.memory_hook: JWT = header.payload.signature. Signed — verify the signature to trust the claims.

terms.definition: Authorisation framework allowing third-party apps to access HTTP services on behalf of a user. OpenID Connect adds authentication (identity) on top.
terms.memory_hook: OAuth 2.0 = 'authorise this app to access your data'. OIDC adds identity on top.

terms.definition: User logs in once to an identity provider (e.g. Keycloak) and accesses multiple applications without re-authenticating.
terms.memory_hook: SSO = one login, everywhere. Enabled by OIDC + Keycloak.

terms.definition: RFC 7617. Browser sends Base64-encoded 'username:password' in the Authorization header. Simple but insecure over plain HTTP — always pair with HTTPS.
terms.memory_hook: BASIC = browser dialog box. No login page. Base64 in the header. HTTPS required.

terms.definition: Container redirects unauthenticated users to an HTML login form. On submit, the container validates credentials and redirects to the original resource.
terms.memory_hook: FORM = custom HTML login page. The most common web login pattern.

terms.definition: Implements IdentityStore. The source of truth for user credentials and roles. Backed by a database, LDAP, or custom store.
terms.memory_hook: Identity Store = the user directory. Validates passwords and provides roles.

terms.definition: An identity layer on OAuth 2.0. Applications redirect to an OIDC provider (Keycloak, Google) for authentication and receive a JWT ID Token with identity claims. Enables SSO.
terms.memory_hook: OIDC = 'Login with Keycloak/Google'. Delegates auth to a trusted provider. Built on OAuth 2.0.

terms.definition: Configures an Identity Store backed by a relational database. Specify datasource, credential validation SQL, and group SQL.
terms.memory_hook: @DatabaseIdentityStoreDefinition = users are in a database table.

terms.definition: Configures an Identity Store backed by an LDAP/Active Directory server.
terms.memory_hook: @LdapIdentityStoreDefinition = users are in Active Directory or LDAP.

terms.definition: Standardises how a web application authenticates HTTP requests. Four built-in implementations: BASIC, FORM, Custom FORM, OpenID Connect. Custom implementations allowed.
terms.memory_hook: HttpAuthenticationMechanism = 'how do I verify who you are?' interface for web apps.

terms.definition: Injectable CDI bean for programmatic security: getCallerPrincipal() (who is logged in), isCallerInRole(role), hasAccessToWebResource(path, method).
terms.memory_hook: SecurityContext = 'who is logged in and what can they do?' programmatically.

terms.definition: Open-source Identity and Access Management server. Supports OIDC, OAuth 2.0, SAML. Issues and validates JWTs, manages users and roles, enables SSO.
terms.memory_hook: Keycloak = the central login server. All your apps authenticate through it.

terms.definition: Arquillian runs the container inside the same JVM as the tests. Fastest — no process startup or network overhead.
terms.memory_hook: Embedded = container in the test JVM. Fastest, no network.

terms.definition: Arquillian starts and stops the container as a separate process. Lifecycle is automatic but slower than embedded.
terms.memory_hook: Managed = Arquillian starts/stops the server as a separate process.

terms.definition: An already-running container is used. Arquillian deploys the archive and runs tests; no lifecycle management.
terms.memory_hook: Remote = connect to an existing server. Arquillian just deploys and tests.

terms.definition: Marks the static method that builds and returns the ShrinkWrap archive deployed before the test methods run.
terms.memory_hook: @Deployment = 'deploy this archive before my tests run'.

terms.definition: JUnit 5 extension integrating Arquillian. Apply with @ExtendWith(ArquillianExtension.class) on the test class.
terms.memory_hook: @ExtendWith(ArquillianExtension.class) = plug Arquillian into JUnit 5.

terms.definition: Container-agnostic integration testing framework for Jakarta EE. Manages container lifecycle and test deployment so developers write plain @Test methods that run in a real container.
terms.memory_hook: Arquillian = integration tests inside a real container. Write @Test; Arquillian handles the server.

terms.definition: Arquillian extension managing Selenium WebDriver instances for browser-based UI testing.
terms.memory_hook: Drone = Arquillian's WebDriver manager. Gets you a browser in your test.

terms.definition: Arquillian extension adding Page Objects and fluent browser automation on top of Drone/WebDriver.
terms.memory_hook: Graphene = Drone + Page Objects. Makes browser tests cleaner.

terms.definition: Lightweight Jakarta EE server for microservices and embedded deployments. Fast startup, small footprint. Used in Arquillian embedded tests.
terms.memory_hook: Payara Micro = tiny Payara. Starts in seconds. Ideal for embedded Arquillian tests.

terms.definition: Fluent Java API for building deployment archives (WebArchive, JavaArchive, EAR) programmatically inside @Deployment methods.
terms.memory_hook: ShrinkWrap.create(WebArchive.class).addClass(MyBean.class) = build the test archive in Java.

terms.definition: Eclipse Foundation open-source umbrella project (started September 2016) with APIs for cloud-native microservices: Config, Health, Metrics, OpenAPI, Fault Tolerance, JWT RBAC, Rest Client, OpenTelemetry.
terms.memory_hook: MicroProfile = cloud-native companion to Jakarta EE. Started 2016.

terms.definition: Externalises config from the artifact. Three mandatory sources (priority order): system properties (400) > env variables (300) > META-INF/microprofile-config.properties (100). Supports dev/prod Config Profiles.
terms.memory_hook: MP Config = inject config from env vars, system props, or .properties file. @ConfigProperty injects values.

terms.definition: Resilience patterns via annotations: @Retry, @Timeout, @Fallback, @CircuitBreaker, @Bulkhead. Annotations can be combined on the same method.
terms.memory_hook: MP Fault Tolerance = annotate methods with resilience. No try/catch boilerplate.

terms.definition: Exposes health check results at the /health HTTP endpoint. For Kubernetes liveness/readiness/startup probes. Annotation types: @Liveness, @Readiness, @Startup.
terms.memory_hook: MP Health = /health endpoint. Kubernetes polls it to decide pod status.

terms.definition: Secures JAX-RS endpoints with RBAC using signed JWTs. Caller presents a JWT; runtime validates the signature and checks the 'groups' claim against @RolesAllowed.
terms.memory_hook: MP JWT = @RolesAllowed + signed JWT. Caller proves identity with a signed token.

terms.definition: Collects application, vendor, and JVM metrics. Exposes at /metrics in JSON or Prometheus format. Annotations: @Counted, @Timed, @Gauge.
terms.memory_hook: MP Metrics = /metrics endpoint. Annotate methods → feed Prometheus → visualise in Grafana.

terms.definition: Auto-generates an OpenAPI v3 specification from JAX-RS annotations. Exposed at /openapi. Swagger UI integration available.
terms.memory_hook: MP OpenAPI = /openapi auto-generated from JAX-RS code. Browse in Swagger UI.

terms.definition: Integrates the CNCF OpenTelemetry SDK. Automatic distributed tracing of REST calls with trace context propagated across services. Exports to Jaeger, Zipkin, Instana.
terms.memory_hook: MP OpenTelemetry = automatic distributed tracing. Traces flow across service boundaries.

terms.definition: Generates a type-safe HTTP client from a JAX-RS-annotated interface. Annotate with @RegisterRestClient; configure base URL in microprofile-config.properties; inject with @Inject @RestClient.
terms.memory_hook: MP Rest Client = write an interface, get an HTTP client for free. Zero boilerplate.

terms.definition: Application architected for the cloud: microservices, containers (Docker), orchestration (Kubernetes), CI/CD pipelines, horizontal scalability.
terms.memory_hook: Cloud-native = microservices + Docker + Kubernetes + CI/CD. Built for the cloud.

terms.definition: Tracking a single request across multiple microservices. Each service records a span. A backend (Jaeger, Zipkin) aggregates spans into a full trace showing the complete call path and timing.
terms.memory_hook: Distributed tracing = follow a request through 10 services and see exactly where time was spent.

terms.definition: Limits concurrent executions of a method. Returns BulkheadException immediately when the limit is reached.
terms.memory_hook: @Bulkhead(value=10) = max 10 concurrent calls. Others fail immediately.

terms.definition: Tracks failure rate; when it exceeds a threshold the circuit opens (fast-fails all calls). After a cooldown it allows one request through to test recovery.
terms.memory_hook: @CircuitBreaker = electrical fuse. Too many failures? Open. Stop trying for a while.

terms.definition: Injects a config value from MicroProfile Config sources into a CDI bean field. @Inject @ConfigProperty(name='server.host') String host.
terms.memory_hook: @Inject @ConfigProperty(name='my.key') String val = inject config into a field.

terms.definition: MicroProfile Metrics annotation. Increments a counter each time the method is invoked.
terms.memory_hook: @Counted = 'how many times was this called?'

terms.definition: Specifies a fallback method or FallbackHandler to invoke when the primary method fails after all retries.
terms.memory_hook: @Fallback(fallbackMethod='myFallback') = call myFallback() when main method fails.

terms.definition: Marks a HealthCheck as a liveness probe. DOWN = tell the container orchestrator to restart this pod.
terms.memory_hook: @Liveness = 'am I alive?' DOWN = restart this pod.

terms.definition: Marks a HealthCheck as a readiness probe. DOWN = stop routing traffic to this instance.
terms.memory_hook: @Readiness = 'am I ready for traffic?' DOWN = take me out of the load balancer.

terms.definition: Marks a JAX-RS-annotated interface as a MicroProfile Rest Client. The runtime generates a proxy. Configure base URL: InterfaceName/mp-rest/url=... in microprofile-config.properties.
terms.memory_hook: @RegisterRestClient = 'generate an HTTP client proxy from this interface'.

terms.definition: CDI qualifier used with @Inject to inject the MicroProfile-generated Rest Client proxy.
terms.memory_hook: @Inject @RestClient MyService svc = inject the generated HTTP client.

terms.definition: Automatically retries a failed method invocation up to a configurable max attempts with optional delay.
terms.memory_hook: @Retry(maxRetries=3) = try up to 3 more times on failure.

terms.definition: Restricts a JAX-RS method to callers whose JWT 'groups' claim includes one of the listed roles. Returns HTTP 403 if the check fails.
terms.memory_hook: @RolesAllowed({'admin'}) = only admins can call this. Others get HTTP 403.

terms.definition: Marks a HealthCheck as a startup probe. DOWN = still initialising. Prevents premature liveness kills during slow startup.
terms.memory_hook: @Startup = 'have I finished booting?' Prevents killing a slow-starting app.

terms.definition: Aborts a method if it does not complete within the specified time. Throws TimeoutException.
terms.memory_hook: @Timeout(value=2, unit=ChronoUnit.SECONDS) = give up after 2 seconds.

terms.definition: Programmatic MicroProfile Config entry point. ConfigProvider.getConfig().getValue('key', String.class).
terms.memory_hook: ConfigProvider.getConfig().getValue('key', String.class) = programmatic config access.

terms.definition: MicroProfile JWT interface. Inject to access the current caller's token claims: getSubject(), getGroups(), getClaim('claimName').
terms.memory_hook: @Inject JsonWebToken jwt; jwt.getSubject() = the caller's ID from the JWT.

terms.definition: Open-source dashboarding platform. Connects to Prometheus and other sources to display metrics as charts and graphs.
terms.memory_hook: Grafana = the dashboard layer over Prometheus. Turn metrics into pretty graphs.

terms.definition: Open-source distributed tracing backend (CNCF). Receives OpenTelemetry traces and provides a UI to visualise trace timelines across services.
terms.memory_hook: Jaeger = the tracing UI. View distributed traces as flame graphs.

terms.definition: IBM's open-source, lightweight, modular Jakarta EE and MicroProfile server.
terms.memory_hook: Open Liberty = IBM's lightweight Jakarta EE + MicroProfile server.

terms.definition: Open-source metrics collection and alerting system. Scrapes /metrics endpoints at intervals and stores time-series data.
terms.memory_hook: Prometheus = metric scraper + time-series DB. Powers Grafana dashboards.

terms.definition: Kubernetes-native Java framework by Red Hat. Implements Jakarta EE and MicroProfile with focus on fast startup, low memory, and GraalVM native image compilation.
terms.memory_hook: Quarkus = Jakarta EE + MicroProfile + native compilation. Millisecond startups.
